title: pavonia urens var. urens
intro: Pavonia urens var. urens, also known as the prickly mallow, is a plant species within the mallow family (Malvaceae) characterized by its pink or purple flowers and prickly leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and glycosides. The health benefits of Pavonia urens var. urens include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat wounds and skin irritations.
